What to Expect from the Reunion
A trailer for the reunion teases some drama.
The tension between Theresa and John is apparent as John says, "I don’t really feel comfortable, like, airing this out."
"Well, that's why we're here," Theresa replies.
"John, is there anything you would like to clear up in this moment?" Joy then asks.
Later in the trailer, John expresses frustration with Theresa, saying, "I don’t deserve to date someone who texts me, ‘I feel disgusting with you.’"
Leah also has an emotional moment, seemingly with Chris, as she says, "You took that from me."
Later in the trailer, Chris says he "wanted to go home" after four days.
Logan addresses Vanessa in another scene, saying, "I mean, we have gone through a lot of s---."
"I don’t want to cry," Vanessa replies.
Vanelle speaks to Jorge as she says, “In my mind, you were literally like my husband."
Theresa later accuses Jorge of "gaslighting" everyone while he's speaking.
Andrew and Libby's relationship takes center stage as Andrew says, "It's not easy," and Libby says she still hasn't met Andrew's daughters.

title: Security Guard at the Center of Chappell Roan Hotel Incident Breaks Silence. Read His Statement
summary: A security guard involved in the Chappell Roan controversy with Chappell Roan has given his side of the story in an Instagram post. Read his full statement.
story: A security guard involved in the controversy with Chappell Roan is speaking out about his role in what happened at a São Paulo hotel earlier this week.

Roan has denied claims by Brazilian soccer star Jorginho Frello that a member of her security detail berated his wife, Irish singer Catherine Harding, and her daughter, Ava, 11, who Harding shares with Jude Law.
Frello, who is known as Jorginho, said a security guard had a “disproportionate” response to the girl walking by Roan’s table at the hotel.
Security guard Pascal Duvier says he finally had to speak up about what happened.
“I do not normally address online rumors, but the accusations currently circulating are false and constitute defamation,” he began an Instagram post on March 25.
Duvier did not shy away from accepting his involvement in the incident, while explaining that he was not associated with Roan.
“I take full responsibility for the interactions on March 21st. I was at the hotel on behalf of another individual, and I was not part of the personal security team of Chappell Roan,” he wrote.

“The actions I took were not on behalf of Chappell Roan, her personal security team, her management, or any other individuals. I made a judgment call based on information we obtained from the hotel, events I had witnessed in the days prior and the heightened overall security risk of our location. My sole interaction with the mother was calm and with good intentions, and the outcome of the encounter is regretful.”
TODAY.com has reached out to Duvier, Roan and Frello for comment.

Frello shared a long message on his Instagram story on March 21, which was then shared on X by Rio de Janeiro Mayor Eduardo Cavaliere, saying that Harding and Ava were coincidentally staying at the same hotel as Roan. He claimed that Ava thought she spotted the Grammy-winning singer while eating breakfast at the hotel, so she walked closer to see if it was her.

Frello said a security guard confronted Harding and her daughter and responded in a way that was “completely disproportionate,” telling Harding that Ava should not “disrespect” or “harass” people. Frello, who had also said Ava and his wife planned to see Roan in concert in São Paulo, said the incident left the girl in tears.
Roan had already said the security guard was not part of her team.

“I’m just going to tell my half of the story of what happened today with a mother and child who were involved with a security guard who is not my personal security,” she said in her own Instagram story in response to Frello’s comments. “I didn’t even see a woman and a child. No one came up to me. No one bothered me. I was just sitting at breakfast in my hotel.”
“I did not ask the security guard to go up and talk to this mother and child,” she continued. “They did not come up to me. They weren’t doing anything.”
Harding gave her side of the story in her own Instagram video on March 22.

“I know that Chappell has responded saying that it wasn’t her security, and she didn’t do it,” she said. “So, 100% this security guard was not a security guard of the hotel, that’s what I can say. He looks after artists. So I don’t know if it was her personal security guard, but he was with her, so that is all I know.”
Roan has addressed fan interaction before.

“I don’t agree with the notion that I owe a mutual exchange of energy, time, or attention to people I don’t know, do not trust, or who creep me out — just because they’re expressing admiration,” she wrote on Instagram in 2024 about being approached when she’s not working.

However, she did add that, “This has nothing to do with the gratitude and love I feel for my community, for the people who respect my boundaries and for the love I feel from every person who lifts me up and has stuck with me to help the project get to where it is now.”

She went on to say she is “specifically talking about predatory behavior (disguised as ‘superfan’ behavior) that has become normalized because of the way women who are well-known have been treated in the past.”
“Please do not assume you know a lot about someone’s life, personality, and boundaries because you are familiar with them and their work online,” she continued.
In her video reacting to Frello’s accusations, though, Roan did say she loves her fans and apologized for what happened.

“I do not hate people who are fans of my music,” she said. “I do not hate children. That is crazy. I’m sorry to the mother and child that someone was assuming something that you would do something, that if you felt uncomfortable, that makes me really sad. You did not deserve that.”

title: John F. Kennedy Jr.'s Style Is Everywhere. But Can He Take Credit for It?
summary: Does JFK JR's fashion sense have roots in Black style of the 90s? Two fashion experts break down the influencers behind John Kennedy's style.
story: On social media, John F. Kennedy Jr. is everywhere.

Not only a person, but as an aesthetic. Across Tiktok, men are recreating his movements through 1990s New York— on a bike in a blazer and backwards cap, in oversized trousers and loafers, in that specific mix of polish and looseness. There's even the suggestion Kennedy's very '90s style should be a blueprint for how men should dress now.
When artist and TikToker Alex Duncan IV first saw promotion for "Love Story," he didn’t take Kennedy's style as a revelation. He saw something familiar.
“I wrote in my notes to make a video about how JFK Jr. just dressed like my uncles,” he tells TODAY.com.

Duncan, a millennial from New York, recognized the silhouette immediately. The Kangol hats, the relaxed tailoring, the habit of pairing sneakers with suits or softening formalwear into something more personal. These were not anomalies in the 1990s; they were part of a broader visual language that existed across Black communities in New York and beyond.
“Anyone that’s Black and lived in America in that time … their dads, their uncles, they all dressed like that,” Duncan explains.

New York City vintage curator and content creator Ned Haan, who has also spoken about Kennedy’s style influence on his Instagram account @neddievintage, traces the same aesthetic lineage not to Hyannis Port or Ivy League prep, but to Harlem in the 1980s, a place where tailoring, sportswear, and experimentation were already being combined in ways that would later be labeled as “timeless.”
“Manhattan adopted it. Harlem created it,” Haan tells TODAY.com.

JFK Jr.’s likely reference points are not obscure. Wesley Snipes in sharply cut suits worn with ease. Denzel Washington, Spike Lee, Tupac, LL Cool J. Figures who moved between refinement and improvisation, treating clothing less as a set of rules and more as a set of possibilities.

Seen through that lens, Kennedy’s style doesn’t disappear, it just shifts position. It becomes part of a continuum rather than the starting point. Or, as Haan puts it, “JFK Jr. was inspired, not the inspiration.”

None of this is particularly controversial within fashion itself. Style has always been iterative, built through observation, influence and reinterpretation. What feels different now is the way those origins are remembered. Or, more often, not remembered at all.

Gen Z’s fixation on Kennedy and Bessette's style arrives at a moment when fashion is being consumed less through physical spaces and more through platforms designed to compress, categorize and redistribute images at scale. It appears as a clip, a template that can be replicated without requiring much knowledge of where it came from or why it worked.

Duncan sees that pull as part of a broader generational pattern. “I think each generation looks back for something to feel connected to,” he says, describing cycles of revival that stretch back decades. “Gen Z are just so digitized. I think they want something that feels unique and organic.”

There is a paradox embedded in that desire. The more people search for something that feels personal, the more they encounter the same references, the same images, the same instructions on how to recreate them. What begins as interpretation can flatten into repetition with surprising speed.

Haan describes seeing versions of Kennedy’s wardrobe replicated almost exactly online. Standardizes outfits assembled from recognizable parts, often without the experimentation that made those combinations compelling in the first place. “There’s a certain sense of not being yourself that comes with this,” he says.
The original style being referenced was not about perfect coordination. “There was no rhyme or reason,” Haan explains. “It just felt good that day.”

The process is harder to access in an environment where feedback is immediate and constant. When every outfit can be measured by engagement, and every aesthetic comes pre-packaged with examples, the incentive shifts away from trial and error and toward approximation.
“When you live in a ChatGPT world,” Haan says, “you don’t feel like you need to learn for yourself through failures and experimentation as much.”

Duncan frames the same phenomenon more bluntly on Substack, describing a “hyper collectivist” culture in which individuality is increasingly difficult to sustain. In his view, the Kennedy revival is less about rediscovery and more about a convergence, a moment where large groups of people arrive at the same aesthetic at the same time, often through the same channels.
That convergence becomes more complicated when questions of authorship enter the picture.

When a style with clear roots in Black culture is widely reintroduced through a white public figure, or a white actor playing a white public figure, the result is a shift in who gets recognized as the source.

Neither Duncan nor Haan argues that Kennedy, or anyone else, should be excluded from participating in that style. In fact, both emphasize that influence across communities is fundamental to how fashion evolves. The issue, instead, is what happens when the chain of influence is shortened or erased altogether.
“Everything comes from somewhere,” Duncan says. “Look up the origins. It’ll help you navigate conversations in the future.”

Haan sees the current moment as an opportunity rather than a dead end. If Kennedy’s resurgence draws attention to a broader aesthetic, it can also serve as a gateway that leads back to the people and places where that aesthetic was first shaped. “Let’s uplift the people who were the real inspiration,” he says.

title: HBO Releases 1st Teaser for 'Harry Potter' Show, Unveils Premiere Date
summary: It's an exciting day for muggles and wizards alike as HBO releases the first teaser trailer for its "Harry Potter" show, premiering Christmas Day 2026.
story: It's an exciting day for muggles and wizards alike.
After months of anticipation, HBO released a teaser trailer for its "Harry Potter" TV series and unveiled its premiere date.
"Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone" will premiere on HBO Christmas Day this year, December 25, 2026.

On March 24, the network shared a sneak peek photo of actor Dominic McLaughlin in costume as Harry wearing a red Gryffindor robe with the No. 7. In its post, HBO teased more information to come on March 25, and with the flick of a wand, HBO granted fans' wishes with the debut of the first official teaser for the show, which promises to be “faithful adaptation" of J. K. Rowling’s books.

The teaser previews the story many have come to love, starting with Harry's life with the Dursleys at 4 Privet Drive. After receiving a letter addressed to the "cupboard under the stairs," Harry is whisked into the magical world of Hogwarts by Hagrid, played in the HBO show by Nick Frost.
In the final scene of the teaser, Harry meets his eventual best friends, Ron and Hermione, played by Arabella Stanton and Alastair Stout, who altogether make up the seven-book series' Golden Trio.
What to Know About the 'Harry Potter' TV Series
In 2023, HBO announced its plans to adapt "Harry Potter" into a TV series. Fans got their first look at the actors playing Harry, Hermione and Ron in May 2025.
Similar to the original seven-book series, HBO plans to make seven seasons of the "Harry Potter" TV series. The first season will premiere at the end of 2026.
In July 2025, the network shared a photo of McLaughlin on set in his Hogwarts uniform, wearing his character's signature round glasses.

“Exploring every corner of the wizarding world, each season will bring ‘Harry Potter’ and its incredible adventures to new and existing audiences,” HBO previously said of the project in a press release.
Rowling is set to serve as executive producer on the project.

title: 'Grey's Anatomy' Longtime Cast Members Kevin McKidd and Kim Raver Leaving Show After Season 22 Finale
summary: As "Grey's Anatomy" prepares to wrap up Season 22 in a few weeks, viewers are learning about a few upcoming cast shakeups. Read what the exiting stars said.
story: As "Grey's Anatomy" prepares to wrap up Season 22 in a few weeks, viewers are learning about a couple upcoming cast shakeups.
On March 25, Variety reported that Kevin McKidd and Kim Raver plan to leave the ABC medical drama at the end of the current season.
The actors play former married couple Dr. Owen Hunt and Dr. Teddy Altman, two Army veterans who recently divorced this season.
The Season 22 finale, which will air on May 7, will mark the last episode for both McKidd and Raver.

McKidd, who first joined the show’s cast in Season 5, called his time on “Grey’s Anatomy” “a huge chapter” of his life and expressed his gratitude for the experience in a statement shared with TODAY.com.

“Playing Dr. Owen Hunt and directing on the show have both shaped me enormously, and I’ve had the privilege of working with an extraordinary group of people throughout that time. I’m particularly thankful to Shonda Rhimes for creating Owen, and for the encouragement she gave me as I stepped into directing,” McKidd said, in part.

Raver first appeared on “Grey’s Anatomy” in Season 6. She left the series after Season 8 and later returned in Season 14. In a statement to Variety, the actor also reflected on her experience on the show.

"Playing Dr. Teddy Altman will always hold a dear and special place in my heart. Sixteen years ago, I had the incredible privilege of stepping into this role thanks to the vision of Shonda Rhimes and Betsy Beers. Since that day, it has been a true gift to bring the iconic words of Shonda and our brilliant team of writers to life," she said, teasing the pair's sendoff.
"Grey's Anatomy" creator Shonda Rhimes applauded the actors' “extraordinary talent, nuance and heart” and said their contributions left "an indelible mark" on the show.

"Over the years, we have had the privilege of watching Owen and Teddy’s love story evolve and deepen- two characters who always seem to find their way back to each other. It is both bittersweet and joyful to give this couple the happy ending their story deserves," Rhimes told Variety in a statement.
"Grey's Anatomy" showrunner Meg Marinis also weighed in on the actors' departures in a statement to the publication, calling McKidd and Raver "cherished members of the Grey’s Anatomy family."

"While their story is coming to a close, this is never truly a goodbye," Marinis said, in part. "We are endlessly grateful for the years, the artistry and the memories they’ve given to the show and we look forward to cheering them on in whatever comes next."

title: Will 'Secret Lives of Mormon Wives' Come Back? Jessi Draper Weighs In
summary: Jessi Draper has opened up about the possible future of "The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives" as authorities investigate multiple domestic violence incidents involving show star Taylor Frankie Paul and ex Dakota Mortensen.
story: Jessi Draper has opened up about the possible future of "The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives" as authorities investigate multiple domestic violence incidents involving show star Taylor Frankie Paul and ex Dakota Mortensen.
Draper spoke with Alex Cooper on the March 25 episode of the "Call Her Daddy" podcast about whether filming of the Hulu reality show may resume in the wake of the allegations against Paul.

"I don’t know because right now we’re on a film pause," Draper said. "I feel like all of us right now are just trying to be like, 'OK,  we’re human beings first and let’s figure that out, and then we’ll get back to filming the show.' Clearly there’s no shortage of drama in our lives, so if we get another season, it’s going to be good."
Filming was halted on the latest season of “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” amid reports there was a domestic violence investigation opened involving Paul and Mortensen.

TMZ later published a video on March 19 appearing to show an altercation between Paul and Mortensen in 2023 that led to her arrest. NBC News has not independently verified the circumstances and authenticity of the video.
In the wake of the video becoming public, Disney canceled her season of "The Bachelorette," which was supposed to premiere on March 22.
Draper told Cooper that she was aware of the video but had never seen it until it was released.
"Obviously back in 2023, we knew what happened," she said.

After a fight with Mortensen in February 2023, Paul was charged with assault, criminal mischief and domestic violence in the presence of a child. Footage from her arrest was featured in Season 1, Episode 1, of "Secret Lives of Mormon Wives." Paul pleaded guilty in abeyance to an aggravated assault charge in August 2023, and the other charges were dropped, according to court records.

The video reportedly from the fight shows Paul appearing to attack Mortensen in front of her daughter. Paul is seen pushing and kicking Mortensen as he repeatedly asks her to stop. It's unclear what transpired before the clip began.

"I don't like to judge someone in their darkest moment," Draper said. "I don't want to judge the person. I'll judge the mistake, and Taylor really has made a lot of changes. And that's what's so hard for me is like this is my real friend, and she does have a really good heart, and I love her so much."

"Seeing that video is like so hard,” an emotional Draper added. "There’s so much pain underneath the surface for Taylor. And I tell her this all the time, it’s not who Taylor is. She got caught in a really bad cycle and just seeing that video — and yes, the child being involved is awful— and everyone involved, is really hard. But Taylor is such a good person, and she’s made some mistakes, and so has Dakota.”
TODAY.com has reached out to Mortensen and reps for Paul for comment on Draper’s interview.

After the release of the video March 19, Paul told NBC News, “I will have my truth.” An unnamed spokesperson for the reality star said, “Releasing an old video, which conveniently omits context, on their son’s birthday is a reprehensible attempt to distract from his own behavior. Thankfully, the public has seen this act before and knows who he is and sadly, many will recognize this pattern of manipulation, both in his actions on the show, and from their own experiences.”

In a statement to People at the time, Mortensen said, “As anyone who has seen the video will understand, this is a deeply upsetting situation. I am, unfortunately, used to these baseless claims about me and our relationship, which I categorically deny. I am focusing on our son and his safety, and hope that Taylor will do the same.”

Draper confirmed filming for the reality show was first paused by production after the cast found out Mortensen had filed a police report. She did not specify when he filed the report and what was alleged in it. A spokesman for the West Jordan, Utah, Police Department told NBC News the department is investigating Mortensen’s allegations of domestic violence from a 2024 incident with Paul. Police in Draper, Utah, said the department is investigating a separate incident of alleged domestic violence involving both parties that was called in on Feb. 24 and 25.
"They paused things for a minute ... then we picked back up," Jessi Draper said of filming.
The cast later unanimously decided to pause filming, she said.

"We just didn’t want to be filming and say the wrong thing on camera not knowing the full story yet," Draper said. "We didn’t want to have to process all this information for the first time and then be speaking about it."
She also said the cast was not fully aware of the issues between Paul and Mortensen.

“No one knew the extent, and Taylor herself has said to us, ‘I didn’t want to tell anyone the extent,’” Draper said. “And I think anyone in that situation knows that it’s so easy just to be like, ‘Everything’s fine.’ I’ve been there, too. So none of us really knew the extent and now finding out all these details, it’s been hard for sure.”
Draper also addressed how the cast may approach talking about everything that's happened if the show resumes filming.

"I think now we’re at a point where we’re like, 'OK, the world knows and like the info’s out there. And so, now we’re good, and we can speak on the things we know, and we can talk about it in a real way. Rather than before we were like, 'We don’t even know if this is going to get out.' So, we didn’t know if we could even talk about it when we were filming," she said. "Now we can."

title: McDonald’s Is ‘Gonna Be Golden’ With ‘KPop Demon Hunters’ Meals — Here’s When
summary: McDonald’s is collaborating with Netflix’s “KPop Demon Hunters,” the Oscar-winning film that has taken the world by storm. Here's when meals come out.
story: The Golden Arches is living up to its nickname with its latest collab.
On March 24, McDonald’s announced it’s teaming up with Netflix’s “KPop Demon Hunters,” the Oscar-winning film that has taken the world by storm.
The partnership that may perhaps rival the “Rick and Morty” Schezuan Sauce craze of yesteryear includes the “first-ever dueling adult meals” inspired by the Saja Boys and HUNTR/X.
This “Battle for the Fans” also includes a new McFlurry flavor, photocards and exclusive fan content, all of which will drop at participating restaurants nationwide on March 31.

“KPop Demon Hunters” is about a South Korean girl group, Huntr/X, that battles demons with music. The Saja Boys, a demon-filled boy band, try to get in their way. The film, whose songs have topped charts, won two Academy Awards: one for the song “Golden” and another for best animated feature film. A sequel is in the works.
What’s on the ‘KPop Demon Hunters’ Menu?

Saja Boys Breakfast Meal: Fans can channel their early-bird hunger to grab this breakfast meal, featuring a Spicy Saja McMuffin with Spicy Saja sauce, a hash brown and a small “Soda Pop” soft drink, all of which nods to the group’s fiery energy.HUNTR/X Meal: This spread includes Ramyeon McShaker Fries, a 10-piece Chicken McNuggets, along with two limited-edition sauces (Hunter Sauce and a purple Demon Sauce), and a medium soft drink. (Ramyeon is a South Korean comfort food consisting of noodles, vegetables and a sachet of powdered soup.)Derpy McFlurry: This à la carte treat, inspired by the “Derpy Tiger” movie character, blends vanilla soft serve with blackberry popping pearls (aka boba) and wild berry sauce.

Saja Boys Breakfast Meal: Fans can channel their early-bird hunger to grab this breakfast meal, featuring a Spicy Saja McMuffin with Spicy Saja sauce, a hash brown and a small “Soda Pop” soft drink, all of which nods to the group’s fiery energy.

HUNTR/X Meal: This spread includes Ramyeon McShaker Fries, a 10-piece Chicken McNuggets, along with two limited-edition sauces (Hunter Sauce and a purple Demon Sauce), and a medium soft drink. (Ramyeon is a South Korean comfort food consisting of noodles, vegetables and a sachet of powdered soup.)
Derpy McFlurry: This à la carte treat, inspired by the “Derpy Tiger” movie character, blends vanilla soft serve with blackberry popping pearls (aka boba) and wild berry sauce.

Every meal will also come with a collectible HUNTR/X or Saja Boys photocard, as well as a “Derpy access card” with a QR code that unlocks exclusive content, like the reveal of the Battle for the Fans winner, via the McDonald’s app.

title: 'NCIS' Kills Off a Major Character in the Show's 500th Episode. The Actor Reacts
summary: “NCIS” star Rocky Carroll talks about his character, Director Vance, being killed off on the show's 500th episode.
story: The 500th episode of “NCIS” definitely wasn’t one to forget.

The long-running CBS series killed off a major character on the March 24 episode when Director Leon Vance, played by Rocky Carroll, died in a shooting with a corrupt agent as part of a plot to shut down NCIS.
After the shooting, Vance has a vision of a young Dr. Ducky Mallard (played by Adam Campbell) before dying. Ducky was an original “NCIS” character played by David McCallum, who died in 2023.
Showrunner Steven D. Binder hatched the idea of killing off Vance on the episode.

“It seems like all hope is gone. But fortunately, Director Vance is able to get to the bottom of this plan and save the agency,” Carroll told USA Today about what Binder told him. “And in the process of saving the agency, he loses his life.”

“I said, ‘Repeat that last part again,’” Carroll said about the conversation with Binder. “I’m sitting there having this out-of-body experience because I’ve been told we’re going to have this episode basically centered around the death of this character. It dawned on me that we’ve just finally come to that road.”
He’s amazed that no one knew Vance’s death was coming.

“I think it’s gonna be a huge shock for people,” he told Variety. I’ve been able to wrap my mind around it and come to terms with it because I’ve known about this since November, when I was first told about what was going to happen.”
Carroll, a veteran actor who has also enjoyed runs on “Chicago Hope,” “The Agency” and “Roc,”  says Vance’s fate was wonderfully done.
“I’ve been able to kind of come full circle with it. And I really think it’s a great episode,” he said.
“NCIS” is currently in its 23rd season and Carroll joined the show during its fifth season in 2008. According to IMDB, he has been on 392 episodes. Carroll marvels at the run he has gotten to enjoy.
“For selfish reasons, I have to remind people: I’ve been a character on this series for 18 years. Most Hollywood careers don’t last 18 years,” he said.

“So to be able to play one character on one of the most popular shows in the world for 18 seasons, that’s the equivalent of living to be 105. You know, if you’ve known somebody who lived to be 105, when you go to that funeral, there’s a part of you that’s like, ‘OK, yeah, this is sad, but I mean, geez! The guy lived to be 105.’ So I feel that way with my character.”
Carroll also appreciates that he got to portray Vance for as long as he did.
“Look, if this had happened in my third season playing this character, I’d be devastated,” he said.

“But if somebody had told me 18 years ago, ‘Here’s the deal: We’re gonna hire you to play this character. We’re gonna give you 18 consecutive seasons of work on the most watched show in the world. At the end of season 18, something’s gonna happen to your character. Will you accept this offer?’ — there’s not a single actor on the planet who wouldn’t say, ‘Oh, hell yeah. Sign me up. Eighteen years of work. Are you kidding me?’”

title: Keith Hernandez Reveals How Much Money He Still Makes In 'Seinfeld' Residuals
summary: Keith Hernandez says how much money he still makes each year for his guest appearance on “Seinfeld.”
story: Keith Hernandez’s guest appearance on “Seinfeld” continues to pay dividends.
The baseball great, who famously played himself on a 1992 episode of the pioneering sitcom, says he still gets paid for the role.
While appearing on the March 24 episode of the “SI Media with Jimmy Traina” podcast, Hernandez was asked how much his last residual check from the show was.
“It varies. I can tell you that on an annual basis what it is,” he said. “It’s close to $5,000 still. It’s the gift that keeps giving.”
“But remember one thing: I was the guest star,” he added.

Hernandez, who now works as an announcer for the New York Mets on SNY, wasn’t just a guest star — he may be the most memorable guest star of the show’s nine-season run. In the two-part episode that aired during the series’ third season, he plays himself and becomes friendly with Jerry, only for Jerry to become jealous when Hernandez starts dating Elaine.

The episode also famously lampooned the movie “JFK" with a storyline in which Kramer and Newman claim Hernandez spit at them after a game in 1987. George, meanwhile, tried to exaggerate his own friendship with Hernandez to continue getting unemployment benefits.
Hernandez, who said he had never watched the series before starring in it, returned to appear in a brief capacity during “Seinfeld’s" 1998 series finale.

He recently crossed paths with Jerry Seinfeld himself, too. Seinfeld was at a Mets spring training game in Port St. Lucie, Florida, when Hernandez walked into an elevator with Seinfeld. Hernandez had no idea Seinfeld was “right next to me” until someone pointed him out.
“I said, ‘Jerry!’” Hernandez recalled, before adding that it was great to see him.

Hernandez is certainly not the only well-known personality to make some extra cash on “Seinfeld.” TODAY’s Al Roker also appeared on “Seinfeld,” although he’s not quite sure his payday is on par with Hernandez’s $5,000 annual take.
“I don’t know if it’s that much,” he said March 25 on TODAY about his role. Al played himself on a 1993 episode during the show’s fifth season.

title: 'Age of Attraction' Finale: Which Couples Stay Together and Who Breaks Up?
summary: Which "Age of Attraction" couples stayed together in the finale? Here's what happens to everyone including John, Theresa, Vanessa, Logan, Pfeifer, Derrick, Leah, Chris and Libby and Adnrew.
story: "Age of Attraction" has taken Netflix viewers on a wild ride while exploring one major question: Does age really matter in a romantic relationship?
The new series follows participants as they date other singles with one simple rule: You can't ask anyone their age until the two of you decide to commit to each other.

In Season 1, six couples decided to move forward with the dating experiment after learning that they had a significant age gap with their new partner. One of the six couples ended up breaking up before decision day, but the remaining five were tasked with deciding whether or not to stay together or go their separate ways.
What happened in the Season 1 "Age Of Attraction" finale? We're recapping the highlights below.
Theresa DeMaria and John Merrill
Ages: John was 27 and Theresa was 54 at the time of filming.
Age gap: 27 years
Do they stay together? Yes
John, a 27-year-old who works in software sales, and Theresa, a 54-year-old stylist, learned that they have a 27-year age gap when they decided to move forward with the dating experiment.

When they moved in together, the couple explored their physical and emotional relationship further and their bond strengthened. However, Theresa was hung up on their age difference at times, especially since she was worried how her children, who are close in age to John, would react.
Heading into decision day, Theresa acknowledged that she had a "huge decision" to make.
“There’s no denying that we had a true and real connection from the beginning. However, I still have some doubts,” she said in a confessional interview.
Meanwhile, John was all-in and hopeful for the future of their relationship.
"I'm fully committed to Theresa and I hope she's ready to take that leap with me," he said in a confessional interview.

When they came face-to-face on decision day, Theresa and John had an emotion discussion. At first, it seemed like Theresa was walking away from their relationship since she began by explaining her reservations about committing to John.
"You're not up for the fight?" he asked after listening to what she had to say.
"I mean, I'm a fighter, I'm also just trying to protect myself and to protect you," she said.
Next, Theresa read John a letter from her children, who said they were excited to see their mom so happy with him.

At the end of their discussion, Theresa said she felt more "a lot more confident and happy" to hear what her children had written. She then told John that she wanted to "walk towards something" with him and pursue their relationship.
Vanessa Drozda and Logan Goodrid
Ages: Vanessa was 49 and Logan was 29 at the time of filming.
Age gap: 20 years
Do they stay together? Yes
Vanessa, a 49-year-old salon owner, ended up connecting with Logan, a 29-year-old who works in corporate purchasing, during the dating experiment.

On decision day, Vanessa was ready to commit to Logan, but he seemed to be undecided. When they met up with each other, Logan highlighted the "ups and downs" and "chaos" of their relationship then said he was worried that he was "not good enough" for Vanessa.

When Logan asked Vanessa for her promise ring back, she was disappointed. Her sadness turned to confusion quite quickly as Logan continued speaking, saying that he realized he didn't have to be “the perfect man.”
"It's been a crazy rollercoaster of a ride but I'm not getting off anytime soon. I love the s--- out of you and I want to make a promise and a commitment," he said.
"What is happening? Vanessa asked as Logan proposed to her.

Vanessa accepted Logan's proposal and reflected on their relationship in a confessional interview, saying, "I came into this experience with a complete open mind and I'm really super happy with how it ended."
Pfeifer Hill and Derrick Fleming
Ages: Pfeifer was 23 and Derrick was 43 at the time of filming.
Age gap: 20 years
Do they stay together? Yes
Derrick, a 43-year-old father of two who works in medical sales, found an instant connection with Pfeifer, a 23-year-old graphic designer, when they first met.

As they got to know each other more and moved in together, things got real for the couple and Pfeifer grew nervous about relocating from Seattle to Dallas, where Derrick lives. With this in mind, Pfeifer had mixed feelings heading into decision day.
“I have confidence in Derrick and I have confidence in our relationship, but I still have a lot of fears about my own future,” she said in a confessional interview.
When the couple met up on decision day, Pfeifer explained the two choices lying ahead of her.

"If I were to leave here without you, I may regret it for the rest of my life. But if we were to stay together, my life would look wildly different than I ever anticipated," she said. "I would be sacrificing my entire life in Seattle: My friends, my support and everything I've built over the last four years."
Ultimately, Pfeifer decided to trust her instincts.
"Today, I'm putting the unknowns aside and I'm trusting my heart. So I am 100% committed to moving to Dallas and starting our journey together outside of all of this," she said.
Derrick was thrilled and said, "I promise I'm always gonna put in the work. I'm always gonna try. I'll always protect you and I'll always protect your heart."
Derrick then handed Pfeifer a key to his home.
"I feel like the luckiest person in the whole world and I have this whole time," she said.
"That makes two of us babe," he said.
Leah Woolfolk and Chris Dahlan
Ages: Leah was 41 and Chris was 26 at the time of filming.
Age gap: 15 years
Do they stay together? No

Flight attendant Leah, 41, was hoping to find a younger husband to match her active lifestyle. She ended up meeting Chris, a 26-year-old public speaker and business owner. When they decided to continue the experiment, the duo learned that they have a 15-year age gap.

While living together, Chris and Leah's personalities clashed a bit. Leah enjoyed spending all her time with her new beau, but he was also fond of his space, so they struggled to find a balance at times.
On decision day, Leah thanked Chris for "hearing" and "seeing" her throughout the experiment.
"I know we've had our fair share of ups and downs. But for me, the good times outweigh the bad. You challenge me to do and be better and I thank you for that," she said.
Meanwhile, Chris thanked Leah for allowing him to feel "honest and open"
"You've challenged me. You've helped me grow. And I hope I've done the same. I can't thank you enough for the experiences that we have lived," he said.
Leah was ready to commit to Chris, but he didn't feel the same way.

"I think we both want to have a family. I think we both want to settle down. I think maybe we want it at different paces. I do think that the age difference did cause an unnecessary pressure on both of us and put us in a situation where for the first time in my life I felt like it was hard to be myself. And that's why ultimately, I feel that this partnership is not right," he said.
Chris went on to thank Leah for "a friendship that I truly value."
Libby Vodicka and Andrew Wheeler
Ages: Libby was 22 and Andrew was 38 at the time of filming.
Age gap: 16 years
Do they stay together? Yes
Andrew, a 38-year-old bar owner and father of two, found a connection with Libby, a 22-year-old social media manager. After committing to each other, the duo learned that they have a 16-year age gap.

When they moved in together, Andrew and Libby had to navigate the opinions of their loved ones. Libby's friends, in particular, weren't sure that she was ready to take on the role of being a stepmother.
On decision day, both Andrew and Libby were emotionally torn.
"Are we too far away from where the other person is in life? I would be lying if I said that that still doesn't cross my mind every single day with Libby," Andrew said in a confessional interview.
Meanwhile, Libby acknowledged that Andrew's reservations had made her question their relationship.

"Andrew's doubts are putting more ideas in my head. I'm like, 'Wait, is he right? Is this going to work?' His doubts have now become my doubts and I think all of this is really starting to freak me out," she said.
When they met on decision day, Andrew said he was wondering if their relationship would work "outside of the bubble that we've been living in."

"You're 22. I'm 38. That's a big gap. Your life is just getting started. You're figuring out who you are, what you want, where you're going. And I'm settled in the life that I've already built. The biggest fear for me is keeping you from your dreams even though you're a part of mine," he said.
Libby explained that Andrew and his daughters would "only be an addition" to her life.
"No matter how much I doubt the age gap, the distance, the timing, I don't doubt the way I feel when I'm with you," Andrew said.
In the end, both Libby and Andrew agreed that they wanted to move forward with their relationship and he invited her to meet his daughters.

title: Emily Bader, Logan Lerman to Star in ‘13 Going on 30’ Reboot
summary: Emily Bader and Logan Lerman are ready to be thirty, flirty and thriving.
story: Emily Bader and Logan Lerman are ready to be thirty, flirty and thriving.
Netflix has announced a reboot of the 2004 romantic comedy “13 Going on 30,” with Bader and Lerman taking over as the leads. The original film starred Jennifer Garner and Mark Ruffalo.

On March 24, the streaming giant tweeted the casting news and announced that Garner will be an executive producer. The “13 Going on 30” reboot will be directed by Bretty Haley, who recently worked with Bader on the hit Netflix flick “People We Meet on Vacation,” based on the Emily Henry novel of the same name.

In a statement about teaming up with Bader again and rebooting the beloved movie, Haley said in a statement, “‘13 Going on 30’ is one of those rare, perfect films. Funny, emotional, deeply human, with unforgettable performances from Jennifer Garner, Mark Ruffalo, and Judy Greer. I’m a longtime fan, so stepping into this reimagining comes with tremendous responsibility. Jennifer Garner being on board as an executive producer, after playing such a big part of what made the original special, is especially meaningful.”

He added, “I also couldn’t be more excited to reunite with Emily Bader after ‘People We Meet on Vacation.’ She and the amazingly talented Logan Lerman are a magical pairing. I feel incredibly lucky to be trusted with something that means so much to so many people.”
At this time, Netflix has not shared information about the reboot’s plot or when it would take place.

The 2004 version is set in 1987 New Jersey, where fans are introduced to 13-year-old Jenna Rink, played by Christa B. Allen. The relatable teenager dreams of becoming popular and being accepted into the “Tom-Tom” clique at her school despite her best friend Matty Flamhaff, played by Sean Marquette, assuring her that she’s better off without them.

On her 13th birthday, Jenna throws a party at her house and Matty gifts her a custom dollhouse that he sprinkles with “magic wishing dust.” After being embarrassed by the cool kids and having her party ruined, Jenna wishes to be “thirty and flirty and thriving,” just like the model she sees on the cover of her favorite magazine. Her wish comes true and she wakes up in the body of a 30-year-old successful magazine editor, portrayed by Garner. But she soon realizes being an adult isn’t as glamorous as she thought it would be. She also wants to rekindle her friendship with adult Matty, portrayed by Ruffalo, after learning the two had a falling out.
Judy Greer, Andy Serkis, Jim Gaffigan and a young Brie Larson also starred in the film.

“13 Going on 30,” featuring a “Thriller” dance sequence, ‘80s fashion and makeup and a Billy Joel needle drop, has continued to delight rom-com fans over the years. Garner has also frequently reflected on her time filming the movie and shared throwback photos from the set on Instagram.
In July 2025, she celebrated Greer, whom she has remained close friends with, turning 50.

“We were friends before taking pictures was a thing, but there is still a little documentation. 266 months of ‘JG’ and 600 months of @missjudygreer. Happy 50th, Judy! I love you,” she captioned a slideshow of pictures that included snaps of her and Greer as Jenna and Tom-Tom.
The post also included a screenshot from 2024 of the two on a video call with Ruffalo when the trio reunited to celebrate the movie’s 20th anniversary.

title: See the 'Moana' Live-Action Actors Side-by-Side with the Animated Stars
summary: Who's playing who in Moana? See the actors side by side with the original actors.
story: The live-action "Moana" is coming this summer, with the stars suited up and ready to prove how far they'll go.
Disney released the first trailer on March 23, giving a closer look at the characters' transformations — and at Dwayne Johnson rocking demigod Maui's long, curly hair.
The movie is set to premiere on July 10, a decade after the Oscar-nominated original, Disney said in a June 2025 press release. The cast began filming in summer 2024.

Auli’i Cravalho, the voice of Moana in the original film, said in a May 2023 Instagram post that she would not play the lead in the live-action film. Now, Cravalho is an executive producer on the live-action project.

“I’m truly honored to pass this baton to the next woman of Pacific Island descent to honor our incredible Pacific peoples, cultures, and communities that help inspire her story, and I look forward to all the beautiful Pacific representation to come. Mahalo,” Cravalho said.
Cravalho told TODAY.com in a January 2024 interview that she felt it was important to find a new lead who was closer to 16, the age of the fictional character.

“I love that the ‘Moana’ universe is growing but when I think of a live-action remake, I think it’s important that that we stay true to character. You find someone who is near to 16, you find someone who has a beautiful singing voice, you find someone who is unknown, you find someone who is of the Pacific islands,” she said. “When I talk about representation, I want to throw the door wide open. I want there to be Pacific Island representation in front as well as behind the camera.”
Catherine Laga’aia, a 17-year-old Australian actor of Samoan heritage, is stepping in to the leading role of Moana, Disney announced in June 2024.
Here’s a look at the 2026 "Moana" live-action characters compared to the 2016 animated film.
Moana (Catherine Laga’aia)
In the trailer, Catherine Laga’aia is dressed in Moana's legendary outfit, accessorized with a flower in her hair and her heart of Te Fiti necklace.
When Disney announced the lead role, Laga’aia reflected on her own heritage and admiration for Moana.
“I’m really excited to embrace this character because Moana is one of my favorites,” the 17-year-old Sydney, Australia, native said in the press release.

“My grandfather comes from Fa’aala, Palauli, in Savai’i. And my grandmother is from Leulumoega Tuai on the main island of ‘Upolu in Samoa," she continued. "I’m honored to have an opportunity to celebrate Samoa and all Pacific Island peoples, and to represent young girls who look like me.”
Maui (Dwayne Johnson)
Johnson, who serves as the original voice of demigod Maui in "Moana" and "Moana 2", makes his return for the live-action film. He is also a producer for "Moana."
He wore a curly-haired wig to recreate Maui's look, and of course replicated his full body tattoos.
When the film was announced, Johnson reflected on his Samoan heritage in a video filmed in Hawaii.
“As many of you know ... we found so much inspiration for Maui in the mana and the presence of my late grandfather, the legendary High Chief Peter Maivia,” Johnson said in a video announcement.
Later, he shared some behind-the-scenes clips from the production on Instagram.
"This means everything to us 🤎 🌊," he wrote.
Chief Tui (John Tui)
Chief Tui, Moana's father, protects and leads Moana's home base of Motunui, a fictional Polynesian island.
Temuera Morrison played Chief Tui in the 2016 animated film, with Christopher Jackson serving as his singing voice.
John Tui, an actor from Auckland, New Zealand, has taken on the role for the live-action film. He's seen in the trailer proudly gazing at Moana as she looks out into the sea for what is yet to come.
Sina (Frankie Adams)
Moana's unwavering mother is played by Frankie Adams in the live-action remake. Nicole Scherzinger was the original voice of Sina.
Upon the film's announcement, Adams posted on Instagram, congratulating Laga’aia and her co-stars.
"So honored and grateful to be part of this team! This is truly surreal. Ok ok I'm off to have another joyful cry and get ready for my trip to Montunui!" she wrote.
Adams is briefly in the live-action trailer, kissing her daughter on the head as Moana embarks on her next voyage.
In an interview with Aloha State Daily in January, Adams reflected on her upcoming movie projects, one of them being "Moana" live-action.

“You must take tissues because the amount you cry — from joy and pride — is quite insane,” Adams said. “I really hope and I feel that people are going to feel really represented when they see that and feel a sense of home.”
Gramma Tala (Rena Owen)
Moana's spiritual mentor, her grandmother, is played by Rena Owen in the live-action movie.
The original voice of Gramma Tala from the 2016 animated film was Rachel House.
From long walks on the beach with her granddaughter to providing wisdom, Gramma Tala spends her last days on Earth to tell Moana, "The Ocean chose you."
Owen shared the trailer on Instagram and wrote: "Can't wait for you all to experience Disney's #moana in theatres July 10! #polynesian #cheehoo 🌴🌀"

title: Why Aren’t Emily Osment, Mitchel Musso in the ‘Hannah Montana’ Special? What the Actors Said
summary: “Hannah Montana” stars Emily Osment and Mitchel Musso missed the 20th anniversary special, but spoke about what the show means to them.
story: The “Hannah Montana” 20th anniversary special is officially here, but two key players from the Disney show are missing in action.

Emily Osment and Mitchel Musso, who starred as Miley Stewart's best friends, Lilly and Oliver, do not appear in the special, which dropped on Disney+ March 24. But they both have spoken out about their experience being part of the hit series to mark 20 years since it premiered in March 2006.
Osment posted an Instagram video explaining that she couldn’t take part in the special because she is shooting her CBS comedy, “Georgie & Mandy’s First Marriage.”

“‘Hannah Montana’ changed my life, it gave me a lifelong respect for this medium of comedy, it taught me discipline, patience, timing and respect working in an adult space so young,” she captioned an Instagram video of her and “Georgie & Mandy’s First Marriage” co-star Montana Jordan on March 23 from the show’s set.
In the video, Osment confirmed she was “not able to be a part of the 20-year reunion because we are here shooting our show." The "Young Sheldon" spinoff is currently airing its second season.
Osment, who after "Hannah Montana" went on to star in "Young & Hungry" and "Young Sheldon," said she feels grateful for all the support she’s received from fans.

“I can’t tell you what your sweet messages mean to me and how lucky I feel to have been apart of this once in a generation goliath of a television show,” she wrote. “Thank you for letting me into your living rooms and I hope to still be there many years from now. Would never be where I am without you guys, working on another beautiful show I love so dearly. With all my heart, THANK YOU.”
Musso, meanwhile, shared a series of photos from his time on “Hannah Montana,” along with a lengthy post detailing his love for the show. He did not share why he was not part of the special.

“‘Hannah Montana’ wrapped around my heart and never really let go. We literally grew up with ya’ll — long days, crazy schedules, learning lines, cracking up between takes, and figuring out life while the cameras rolled,” he wrote.
“It taught me so many wonderful values, but the most important ‘to me’ is that laughter can get you through the tough days. That confidence still sticks with me every single day.”
Musso, who in later seasons played Lilly's love interest on the show, referenced Osment's post while noting how gratifying it is to see their viewers grow into adults.

“To all of you who grew up right alongside us… your friends were our friends too. Like my Lillypop said on her post, so many of you building families of your own and sharing this wonderful story with your kids is a gift beyond words. It’s been the coolest part of HM,” he wrote.
Musso again acknowledged Osment, as well as Miley Cyrus, Billy Ray Cyrus and fellow cast members Moises Arias and Jason Earles.

“Miley, Emily, Jason, Billy Ray, Moises, and the whole crew at Disney — you became my family through all the real, messy, beautiful moments. Thank you for believing in a goofy kid from Texas who wore some questionable outfits with a haircut like that,” he wrote.
“To all of you, thank you for welcoming me into your homes and hearts 20 years ago and still allowing me to be here today. I’m honored we get to share all of this together.”
CORRECTION (March 25, 2026, 7:30 a.m.): An earlier headline on this article misspelled Mitchel Musso's first name.

title: 'Bridgerton' Reveals Season 5 Will Spotlight Its 1st Leading Queer Couple. What Happens In Francesca's Book?
summary: What happens in Francesca's season of Bridgerton and the book by Julia Quinn? Book spoilers give away what happens next.
story: The star of "Bridgerton" Season 5 is officially Francesca, Netflix revealed March 24, marking the first season led by a queer couple  and another major departure from the order of the Julia Quinn books that inspired it.

Season 5 is officially in production, the streamer announced on X, accompanied by a teaser video revealing that the two romantic leads will be Hannah Dodd's Francesca Bridgerton and Masali Baduza's Michaela Stirling.
"A certain countess shall find love again," the caption teased.
In the clip, Francesca and Michaela stand together on a balcony overlooking the countryside, and after sharing a glimpse at each other, gently touch hands.

"Bridgerton" showrunner Jess Brownell previously confirmed that the already renewed Seasons 5 and 6 will be led by Eloise and Francesca, but she didn't confirm which character pertains to which season.

Now, Francesca's season is officially leapfrogging that of the character's elder sister Eloise, played by Claudia Jessie. In Quinn's "Bridgerton" books, Eloise's story is told in Book 5, "To Sir Phillip With Love," followed by Francesca's in Book 6, "When He Was Wicked."
This isn't the first time the show has gone out of order. While Benedict's season was told in Season 4, he's the star of Book 3, "An Offer From a Gentleman."

But this is the first time the show's leading couple will be queer, a change fans have known since the Season 3 finale introduced a gender-swapped version of Francesca's love interest from the book, Michael Stirling, as Michaela Stirling. The new couple already has a ship name, hailed by Netflix itself: "Franchaela."
Here's what to know about Season 5, including spoilers from the book.
What Will Happen in Season 5 of 'Bridgerton'?
Warning: This section contains spoilers for the ending of "Bridgerton" Season 4.
Season 5 will pick up two years after the death of Francesca's husband, John Stirling (Victor Alli), who suddenly died in Season 4, Episode 6.

After his death, Francesca was distraught and leaned heavily on Michaela, John's cousin. Though their relationship started off rocky, with Dodd previously telling TODAY.com that Michaela “stirs something up” in Francesca, they mended fences after having a candid conversation about their differences and their shared love for John.
After his death, Francesca asks Michaela to stay in London with her as the search begins for the next heir to the Kilmartin line.

“If you would like it, I shall stay,” Michaela responds with a smile. But after Francesca grabs her hand and says, “I feel very close to you, Michaela,” Michaela pulls away, and in the next scene, has suddenly departed London.
“She has packed her belongings and left,” a maid tells Francesca, marking how both characters were left off in Season 4.
In Season 5, though, Francesca will find herself in search of a new partner, for "practical reasons," after navigating her grief. Then, Michaela will return.

The official synopsis reads, “Two years after losing her beloved husband John, Francesca (Hannah Dodd) decides to reenter the marriage mart for practical reasons. But when John’s cousin Michaela (Masali Baduza) returns to London to tend to the Kilmartin estate, Fran’s complicated feelings will have her questioning whether to stick to her pragmatic intentions or pursue her inner passions.”
What Happens In Francesca's Book, 'When He Was Wicked'?
Warning: This section contains spoilers to Julia Quinn's "When He Was Wicked," the inspiration for the forthcoming Season 5 of "Bridgerton."

The beginning of "When He Was Wicked" establishes some of the events viewers have already seen play out on screen: Francesca, deeply in love with her husband, has her heart broken by his sudden death.

In the book, Michaela Stirling is Michael Stirling, and after his cousin's death and after Francesca is confirmed not to be pregnant, he becomes the Earl of Kilmartin. But he has a secret: He's been in love with Francesca since he first met her, at a party celebrating her engagement to John.
Wanting to avoid her and his complicated feelings, Michael leaves.
Four years later, Francesca decides she wants to get re-married just so she can have a child, thinking she already had her great love with John.
At this time, she reconnects with Michael, and they have a yearning, push-pull dynamic defined by attraction, seduction and heaps of guilt as Francesca finds herself falling for Michael.
After several steamy encounters, they decide to marry. In the final scene, Francesca visits John's grave and finally admits she loves Michael, which he overhears, sealing their happily ever after.

title: Mel Schilling, Star of ‘Married at First Sight,’ Dies at 54 After Revealing Terminal Colon Cancer
summary: Mel Schilling, the dating coach on the British and Australian versions of “Married at First Sight,” has died. She'd been diagnosed with colon cancer.
story: Mel Schilling, who appeared as a dating coach on the British and Australian versions of “Married at First Sight,” has died, her family announced. She was 54.

In one of her last social media posts earlier this month, Schilling revealed the colon cancer she'd been diagnosed with in 2023 could no longer be treated. The tumor, which was “the size of a lemon,” was discovered during a scan, she said. It was removed, but the disease later returned and spread to her lungs and brain.
On her Instagram page on Tuesday, March 24, her husband wrote that she passed away peacefully and surrounded by love. Schilling is also survived by her 10-year-old daughter, Madison.

“In her final moments, when I thought cancer had taken away her ability to speak, she ushered me closer and whispered a message for Maddie and me that will sustain me for the rest of my life,” her husband, Gareth Brisbane, wrote.

“This is a woman who, through two years of chemotherapy, when she could barely lift her head from the pillow, never complained and never stopped showing courage, grace, compassion and empathy, and never missed a day of filming.”
He added that his wife became a new mom and a TV star at 42 “and nailed both.”

“Life can be beautiful, and life can be incredibly cruel. But ultimately, life is fleeting, fragile, and tomorrow is promised to no one. If you can do anything to honour Mel, please live life to the full, love your people well, and try not to sweat the small stuff,” Brisbane wrote.
“I had 15 wonderful years with my soulmate, and it was the privilege of my life to be by her side. For that, I will be forever thankful.”
Schilling’s colon cancer tumor, which she called “Terry,” was initially successfully removed, but the cancer returned and metastasized to her lungs, she revealed.

When she began experiencing headaches and numbness on her right side, tests showed the cancer had spread to the left side of her brain. Schilling said her oncology team told her “there is nothing further they can do."
“If I could leave you with one thing, it would simply be this: if something doesn’t feel right, please get it checked out. It might just save your life,” Schilling wrote.
Her colon cancer symptoms included severe stomach cramps, and Schilling was initially told she had constipation before a scan revealed the tumor, the BBC reported.

title: Where Do Miley Cyrus and Her Dad Billy Ray Cyrus Stand Now?
summary: How Miley Cyrus' complicated relationship with dad Billy Ray Cyrus has evolved over the years and where they are now.
story: Miley Cyrus' complicated relationship with dad Billy Ray Cyrus has made plenty of headlines over the years, but it seems the two have mended their relationship so much they've become nostalgic about their past.
The two entertainers will reunite onscreen in an hourlong “Hannah Montana” 20th anniversary special airing on Disney+ on March 24.

The pair’s onscreen reunion comes after Miley Cyrus reportedly stopped speaking to her father for several years in the midst of her parents’ 2022 divorce, according to an interview she gave to Variety earlier this year.
In February 2024, the 33-year-old "Flowers" singer declined to thank her father when she won her first two Grammy awards.
She later acknowledged the pair had "challenges over the years" in a statement on Instagram in May 2025, but said her family had now become her "priority above all else."
Then, in September 2025, Miley Cyrus surprised fans when she released an emotional song, “Secrets,” as a “peace offering” to her once estranged father, per an Instagram caption.
The younger Cyrus told NPR at the time the song acted as "musical medicine" to help father and daughter repair their tattered bond.
“It really helped build a bridge between him and I and reconnecting with each other,” she said.
Ahead of Miley Cyrus and Billy Ray Cyrus’ onscreen reunion later this month, let’s take a look back at their close and complicated relationship.
November 1992: Miley Cyrus Is Born the Same Year Her Dad Has a No. 1 Song

Billy Ray Cyrus and then-wife Tish Cyrus welcomed Miley Cyrus on Nov. 23, 1992, the same year Billy Ray Cyrus' hit song, “Achy Breaky Heart,” became the No. 1 song on Billboard's Hot Country Songs chart in the United States.

Billy Ray Cyrus and Tish Cyrus went on to welcome a son, Braison, and another daughter, Noah, together. Billy Ray Cyrus also adopted Tish Cyrus' children Brandi Cyrus and Trace Cyrus from her earlier marriage to Baxter Neal Helson.
March 2006: ‘Hannah Montana' Premieres on the Disney Channel

After appearing in a few small roles, including guest spots on Billy Ray Cyrus’ medical drama “Doc,” Miley Cyrus found fame alongside her dad when the pair co-star together on the hit Disney series “Hannah Montana,” which aired from 2006 to 2011.

The series told the story of a fictional teenager named Miley Stewart (Miley Cyrus) who lived a double life as a pop star named Hannah Montana. The character’s father, Robby (Billy Ray Cyrus), was a retired country music singer who wrote all her hits and managed her career.

According to Variety, toward the end of the show’s run, Billy Ray Cyrus’ relationship with Miley Cyrus’s mom, Tish Cyrus, had begun to unravel, which caused his relationship with his daughter to fray.
February 2011: Billy Ray Cyrus Says 'Hannah Montana' Drove a 'Wedge' Between Him and Miley Cyrus
In a candid interview with GQ published in February 2011, Billy Ray Cyrus said co-starring with his daughter in "Hannah Montana" for four seasons took a toll on their relationship.
“The business was driving a wedge between us,” he said.
He also said he found it difficult to discipline his kids and later blamed himself for Miley Cyrus' highly publicized post-"Hannah Montana" rebelliousness.

“How many interviews did I give and say, ‘You know what’s important between me and Miley is I try to be a friend to my kids’? I said it a lot. And sometimes I would even read other parents might say, ‘You don’t need to be a friend, you need to be a parent.’ Well, I’m the first guy to say to them right now: You were right.'

"I should have been a better parent. I should have said, ‘Enough is enough — it’s getting dangerous and somebody’s going to get hurt.’ I should have, but I didn’t. Honestly, I didn’t know the ball was out of bounds until it was way up in the stands somewhere.”
Miley Cyrus, in a 2026 interview with Variety, credited her dad with "protecting her" on the set of the show.
“My dad was on set every single day,” Cyrus said. “So there was nothing that could happen that he wouldn’t know about.”
April 2022: Billy Ray Cyrus and Tish Cyrus Announce Divorce

After both she and Billy Ray Cyrus previously filed for divorce and then changed their minds, Tish Cyrus filed for divorce a third and final time in April 2022, according to court documents obtained by NBC News at the time.
The Cyrus family matriarch cited irreconcilable differences as grounds for the divorce and stated she and Billy Ray had not lived together for more than two years.
August 2023: Miley Cyrus Walks Tish Cyrus Down the Aisle When She Remarries
Miley Cyrus walked Tish Cyrus down the aisle when she tied the knot with "Prison Break" star Dominic Purcell in August 2023, Vogue reported.
“To see my mom this happy and in love is very emotional for me,” Miley Cyrus told the magazine. “Dom and my mom share the sweetest, most genuine love. It’s almost like it’s for the first time... "
"To stand beside my mother and give her away to Dom," she added, "is a moment I will cherish for the rest of my life.”
October 2023: Miley Cyrus Skips Billy Ray Cyrus' Wedding with Firehose
When Billy Ray Cyrus married Australian musician Firehose in October 2023, Miley Cyrus did not attend the ceremony.
The couple split less than a year later.
February 2024: Miley Cyrus Does Not Mention Billy Ray Cyrus in Grammy Speech
In February 2024, Miley Cyrus won the first two Grammys of her music career. While accepting the award for record of the year, she declined to thank her father in her speech.
The singer gave credit to her collaborators and her team along with her “main gays because look how good I look!”
She also thanked her mom, her sister, and her now-fiancé Maxx Morando but did not mention Billy Ray Cyrus in her speech.
“All the people that we love. I don’t think I’ve forgotten anyone,” she concluded. “But I might have forgotten underwear. Bye!”
The singer also did not mention her dad in a follow-up post about the Grammys she shared on Instagram.
June 7, 2024: Billy Ray Shares One of His 'Best Memories' with Miley Cyrus
In June 2024, Billy Ray Cyrus shared a sweet throwback pic of him  holding Miley Cyrus as a baby on Instagram in honor of the CMA Fest.

“One of my best memories ever: @cma Fest, back when it was Fan Fair. That’s @mileycyrus and me, surrounded by thousands of incredible fans at the fairgrounds,” he wrote alongside the pic and images of a handwritten poem he wrote while at the musical festival.

"The next day, someone handed me this picture, and I wrote that poem right then on the bus," Cyrus continued before adding of his daughter, "I’m incredibly proud of her. She’s a survivor and a true artist. She learned early on to love and appreciate the fans who make everything possible.
"We both cherish the connection we have with our fans and are grateful for every single one of you!"
June 12, 2024: Miley Cyrus Credits Her Dad with Teaching Her How to Handle Fame
During a lengthy interview on David Letterman’s Netflix show, "My Next Guest Needs No Introduction," Miley Cyrus credited her dad with teaching her how to navigate stardom.
When asked if her dad was her hero, the singer demurred, calling her mother her hero — but said she was grateful to inherit his "genes."
"My dad has great hair and I got that," she said.
She added that she her dad had  "a foot on the ground" and still loves the "real," and "nature," she said, clasping her fist. "And he always did even when he was super famous."
“I’m grateful for being able to watch him ahead of me. He’s almost given me this map. And there’s a map of what to do and what not to do, and he’s guided me on both," she said.
She later called her dad a "creative" and an "artist in the way that his brain works." She said he "has always made me feel safer in my own mind because we’re very similar in our ideas."
“So I think a lot of his perspective on reality and on life, I’ve inherited from him, more so than the way that I was raised —which really, my mom raised me.”
May 9, 2025: Billy Ray Cyrus Shares Surprise Pic with Miley Cyrus
Billy Ray Cyrus surprised fans when he shared a photo featuring Miley Cyrus and Maxx Morando in honor of his son Braison Cyrus' birthday.
May 10, 2025: Miley Cyrus Addresses 'Challenges' in Relationship with Billy Ray Cyrus
Miley Cyrus addressed the speculation about her her complicated relationship with her dad in a statement in her Instagram story on May 10, 2025.

“My dad and I have had our challenges over the years. Now, in my thirties, family is my priority above all else,” she wrote at the time. “I’m at peace knowing bridges have been built and time has done a lot of healing. Grateful for the good health and love that flows through my family.”
May 31, 2025: Miley Cyrus Says Her Mom’s  ‘Hurt’ Affected Her Own Relationship with Billy Ray Cyrus
The same month, when Miley Cyrus spoke to The New York Times, she indicated that the “hurt’ her mother had suffered in her marriage to her father affected her feelings about him.

“As I’ve gotten older, I’m respecting my parents as individuals instead of as parents — because my mom’s really loved my dad for her whole life, and I think being married to someone in the music industry and not being a part of it is obviously really hard,” she said.
“And so I think I took on some of my mom’s hurt as my own because it hurt her more than it hurt me as an adult, and so I owned a lot of her pain.”
She added that it helped her to see her mother happy in her marriage to actor Dominic Purcell and her father in a new romance with model and actor Elizabeth Hurley.

“Now that my mom is so in love with my stepdad, who I completely adore, and now that my dad, I see him finding happiness, too — I can love them both as individuals instead of as a parental pairing,” she explained. “I’m being an adult about it.”
September 19, 2025: Miley Cyrus Releases 'Peace Offering' Song About Billy Ray Cyrus
Miley Cyrus shared a new song about mending her relationship with her dad called “Secrets,” on Instagram.
"This song was written as a peace offering for someone I had lost for a time but always loved. In my experience, forgiveness and freedom are one and the same,” she wrote.

In the song, which features musicians Lindsey Buckingham and Mick Fleetwood of Fleetwood Mac, which Billy Ray Cyrus has called his favorite band, Miley Cyrus sings, “Call off all your forces / A white flag in the war.”
The same month, the singer told NPR, “I wanted (Billy Ray Cyrus) to know that I’m grown now, and I’m a safe space for him. I’m as much a friend as I am his kid now."

“That’s what this song was really about for me," she added. "Being that place that my dad feels safe… if you don’t take my hand as I reach it out, I’m going to chase you because I want that relationship in my life.”
The gesture worked. “It really helped build a bridge between him and I and reconnecting with each other,” she said.
September 19, 2025: Billy Ray Cyrus Responds to Miley Cyrus' Song About Him

After hearing "Secrets," Billy Ray Cyrus responded on Instagram, writing, “Best birthday gift a dad could ever ask for,” he wrote in an Instagram post. “The memories we’ve made together mean the world to me, and seeing you soar with your music makes me prouder every single day.”

title: 'The Pitt' Star Shawn Hatosy Says 'Nuanced' Dynamic Between Abbot and Mohan Was Planned From the 'Get Go'
summary: Shawn Hatosy speaks about season 2 of The Pitt, his character Jack Abbot and his dynamic with Mohan and more.
story: Shawn Hatosy made a heart-stopping entrance in Season 2 of "The Pitt" hours before his character's designated shift start time.
Dressed in a full S.W.A.T. uniform, Hatosy's character, Dr. Jack Abbot, stormed into the Pittsburgh Trauma Medical Center caring for a fallen comrade while nursing a graze wound of his own.

It's a far cry from his character's introduction in Season 1, when he's found standing on the roof of the hospital at the end of the night shift, requiring Dr. Robby (Noah Wyle) to quite literally talk him off the ledge.
Or is it?

"I believe Abbot is in the process of doing the work," Hatosy tells TODAY.com of his character in Season 2, which is set during a Fourth of July shift. "I imagine he had a conversation with his therapist ... and the therapist said, 'You need a hobby.'"
"From that, he decided that being a SWAT medic was appropriate, and he believes he's in the process of doing the work, but in many ways, it's a death wish," he adds.

Speaking with TODAY.com after Episode 11 of "The Pitt" aired March 19, Hatosy previews what's ahead for his character in Season 2, that "flirtatious" scene between Dr. Mohan and Dr. Abbot and his role opposite Sarah Michelle Gellar in "Ready or Not 2: Here I Come."

Abbot's approach to to leadership is so different to Robby's. You know, it's such a fascinating character because his presence is always kind of lingering, even though, to be quite honest, he's not there that much, right? It's a fantastic part. I get to show up, contemplate my existence, and then flirt sometimes, and be charming. And then when the chaos is hitting, he's just this calming influence. So I really enjoy the way these characters are drawn. They're kind of mirrors. They're very similar in stature. But they challenge each other.

That's a good point about McKay, because, she's certainly a character who is prime for — in my opinion, I don't know what they're thinking — a leadership role. You know what I mean, she could. It would be a different approach to to how they handle things absolutely.

I believe Abbot is in process of doing the work. You know? I imagine he had a conversation with his therapist — which he's been trying to get Robby to talk to, as we learned at the end of Season 1 — and the therapist said, 'You need a hobby. You need go play golf, go play pickleball or something.' And he from that decided that being a SWAT medic was appropriate. He believes he's in process doing the work. But in many ways, it's a death wish.

He’s so addicted to the adrenaline that it’s not really too different from what we’re seeing with Dr. Robby. It is interesting how he comes in. I remember as Season 1 ended, I wasn’t totally sure what they had in mind for Abbot in Season 2. I didn’t know, maybe they were going to bring him in full time, and at a certain point, I talked to Scott Gemmill, our showrunner, and he said, ‘Well, we think you’re going to come back around Episode 7,’ and I was terrified because I’m the night shift attending, I should be handing things off. I thought, oh gosh, it’s Fourth of July, how is Abbot going to show up? Is he on a gurney? Did he jump off the roof? A lot of questions were asked.

The writers from the get go, were layering subtle (hints of) that dynamic from the beginning, and so it was definitely discussed. But I think that the scene in Episode 7, where they're in the room together, and he's fixing himself, I just really enjoyed how that dynamic is nuanced and subtle, and it really asks more questions than it answers.

I really enjoy how she says something, and he says, "No, don't worry about me." And she's not. (Laughs.) She's just worried about her patient, which is what her DNA is. She's in a tough spot, and he's, in his own Abbot way, providing help and leadership.
I think that Dr. Abbot would say to Mohan, "Are you crazy? You're the future of medicine."
It's a bloody good time.

There's a dysfunction to it, but it's so enjoyable and delicious. There's tons of blood, there's suspense, there's mystery, there's this game that they're playing where they're trying to kill Grace (Samara Weaving) and Faith (Kathryn Newton). And there's characters that have a lot of different point of views. Mine being one who, you know, he's a bit of an imbecile. He comes from a family that has a lot of wealth and power and that created this very insufferable, infantile human who sort of has been given everything that he's ever wanted and anything that wasn't given to him, he took. And as power is dangled to him, the opportunity to have it for himself, we see how far he'll go and and who he becomes.

It's exciting, right? As actors, we love to be challenged, and we get the opportunity to display different dimensions, and these two are polar opposites, so it's fun. I'm really excited about "Ready or Not 2." I got to see it with an audience, which I encourage everyone to do, because it's nice to get back into a cinema and have that experience with a community of people who like that kind of film.

title: Hallmark Is Going on Tour! See the Dates and Stars Hitting the Road
summary: Hallmark stars are going on tour this summer! See tour dates, the stars attending and how to get tickets.
story: Hallmark fans are in for a treat! The network will soon embark on its first-ever tour, where attendees will get to meet the stars from some of their favorite TV movies in person.

“Hallmark Stars Live” will launch this summer, the network and Gershwin Entertainment announced March 24 on TODAY. The tour will be hosted by “A Grand Ole Opry Christmas” actor Nikki DeLoach and “feature Hallmark mainstays for a night where the cameras are off — and the stories keep rolling,” according to a press release. The retailer Michaels is also a presenting partner for the tour.

DeLoach and other stars will hop from city to city for two weeks — from July 22 to Aug. 2 — where they will bring “a little Hallmark movie magic” to the audience during a 90-minute event. But unlike Hallmark’s films, the events will be entirely unscripted. At each of the 10 stops, the stars will share behind-the-scenes stories from their time on set and answer questions from fans.

“At Hallmark, we’re always exploring new ways to connect with fans and bring our brand to life, so we’re thrilled to introduce ‘Hallmark Stars Live’ as our newest immersive experience,” said Lindsey Roy, senior vice president of branded experiences at Hallmark. “For everyone who’s asked us to bring the Hallmark joy they love a little closer to home, this tour is for you.”
Read on to find out everything you need to know about the "Hallmark Stars Live" tour.
Which Stars Are Joining the Hallmark Tour?
